title: Slow-Roasted Squash with Maple Bourbon Glaze
summary: We’ve taken butternut squash to the next level with a sticky maple syrup and whiskey glaze. An incredible side dish.

method.description: Preheat the oven to 175°C / 350°F. Peel and deseed the butternut squash and cut it into wedges.
Toss the squash wedges with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Arrange on a baking sheet and roast for 25-30 mins until softened.
To make the glaze, set a small saucepan over a low-medium heat. Add the maple syrup, bourbon and cinnamon. Stir to combine and simmer until slightly thickened.
Brush the roasted squash with the maple bourbon glaze. Return to the oven and roast for an additional 10 mins. Meanwhile, toast the pumpkin seeds in a dry frying pan.
To serve, arrange the squash on a platter and sprinkle with toasted pumpkin seeds.

chefs.name: Laurent Dagenais
chefs.summary:
Born and raised in Montreal, Dagenais is a chef by training who worked in the restaurant industry for almost a decade before a snowboarding accident changed his trajectory. “A decade ago my career took a turn that I would have never predicted,” explained Dagenais. “Breaking my arm put my cooking life on hold and that's why, in the midst of the pandemic, I started making videos on the internet.”
 
It was these videos that changed his life, making him an instant social media phenomenon. Reaching over 4+ million followers across his channels, Dagenais' main goal is to share his love of food and inspire people to get in to the kitchen more often.
